name: Jay McShann
description: American blues, jazz, and swing bandleader, pianist and singer (1916–2006)
abstract: James Columbus "Jay" McShann (January 12, 1916 – December 7, 2006) was an American jazz pianist, vocalist, composer, and bandleader. He led bands in Kansas City, Missouri, that included Charlie Parker, Bernard Anderson, Walter Brown, and Ben Webster.

interviews.title: Jay McShann interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I knew Al but I didn't know all that about him... Bird actually says that the reason that he — when he visited New York with Jay McShann back in the early ‘40s... he heard a new form of music being played under the leadership of a pianist named Allen Tinney.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee Sam Jones is not mentioned in the transcript; the text only contains the interviewer discussing Jay McShann's relationship with Bird (Charlie Parker).

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: His arrangements for the Sweet Baby Blues Band capture the Kansas City jump blues style and make the seven-piece band swing like Jay McShann and Count Basie.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Freddie Green
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Freddie Green) does not speak in the provided context. The mention of Jay McShann is made by the Interviewer in relation to Charlie Parker.

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: You did another tour of Europe in 1970 with Jay McShann. Was it fun playing with Jay? Oh yeah, talk about fun-- he and George Kells, he's my buddy.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: I was to meet and work with a famous musician... Jay played and sang... when it came to accompanying me on my numbers
